---
title: "Notes Feature"
---

## Candidate Notes System

Persistent note-taking for candidate tracking and collaboration.

---

## Overview

The notes feature allows you to:
- ✅ Add detailed notes to any candidate
- ✅ Rate candidates (1-5 stars)
- ✅ View all notes in one place
- ✅ Export notes to CSV/JSON
- ✅ Track review history with timestamps

---

## How Notes Are Stored

### Storage Location

Notes are stored in **browser localStorage** under the key:

```javascript
'candidate_matcher_notes'
```

### Data Structure

```json
{
  "candidate_id": [
    {
      "note_text": "Strong Python skills, lacks AWS experience...",
      "rating": 4,
      "created_at": "2026-05-29T12:00:00.000Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-05-29T12:00:00.000Z"
    },
    // ... more notes for this candidate
  ],
  // ... more candidates
}
```

### Implications

**✅ Advantages:**
- No server required
- Instant save (no network delay)
- Works offline
- Private (never leaves your browser)
- Persists across sessions

**⚠️ Limitations:**
- Not synced across devices
- Lost if browser cache cleared
- No backup unless exported
- Per-browser storage (~5-10MB limit)

---

## Adding Notes

### Step-by-Step

1. **Open candidate modal**
   - Click on candidate card from results
   - Or search and select from list

2. **Scroll to Notes section**
   - Below profile and skills
   - Text area labeled "Add your notes here..."

3. **Write your note**
   - Be specific and actionable
   - Include concerns, strengths, next steps
   - Reference job requirements if relevant

4. **Add rating (optional)**
   - Select 1-5 stars from dropdown
   - Helps prioritize later
   - Not required

5. **Click "Save Note"**
   - Note appears in list below immediately
   - Shows timestamp
   - Can add multiple notes per candidate

### Example Notes

**Good examples:**

> "Excellent Python and Django skills. Lacks AWS experience but has GCP - should transfer easily. Salary expectation ($150k) is within our $140-180k range. Available in 2 weeks. Recommend technical screen." ⭐⭐⭐⭐

> "Red flag: Job hopped 3 times in 2 years. Strong technical skills but may not stay long-term. Consider for contract role instead of full-time." ⭐⭐

> "Perfect culture fit - startup experience, wears many hats. Skills match 85%. Can start immediately. Let's fast-track to final round!" ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

**Avoid:**

> "Good candidate." ❌ (too vague)

> "Has Python, AWS, Docker." ❌ (just restating resume)

---

## Viewing Notes

### Notes Viewer Page

Access via:
- Click **"📝 View All Notes"** link in header
- Or go directly to `/notes.html`

**Features:**
- All notes from all candidates
- Sorted by newest first
- Full candidate context displayed
- Statistics dashboard

### Statistics Dashboard

Shows at top of notes page:

| Metric | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Total Notes** | Count of all saved notes |
| **Candidates with Notes** | Unique candidates reviewed |
| **Average Rating** | Mean rating (only rated notes) |

### Note Card Display

Each note shows:
- Candidate name, title, company
- Location, years experience
- Email (if available)
- Skills tags (first 5 + count)
- Rating (stars)
- Full note text
- Timestamp

---

## Exporting Notes

### CSV Export

**What you get:**
```csv
Candidate ID,First Name,Last Name,Email,Current Title,Company,Location,Years Experience,Skills,Rating,Note,Created At
123,Jane,Doe,jane@email.com,Senior Engineer,TechCorp,"New York, NY",8,"Python;AWS;Docker",4,"Strong candidate...",2026-05-29T12:00:00Z
```

**Use cases:**
- Share with hiring team
- Import into spreadsheet for analysis
- Backup before clearing cache
- Archive for compliance

### JSON Export

**What you get:**
```json
{
  "exported_at": "2026-05-29T12:00:00Z",
  "total_notes": 15,
  "notes": [
    {
      "candidate": { ... full candidate data ... },
      "notes": [ ... all notes for this candidate ... ]
    }
  ]
}
```

**Use cases:**
- Developers/programmatic access
- Re-import later (manual process)
- Complete backup with structure
- Integration with other tools

### How to Export

1. Go to notes page (`notes.html`)
2. Click **"📊 Export CSV"** or **"📄 Export JSON"**
3. File downloads automatically
4. Save to desired location

**Best practice:** Export weekly as part of your routine.

---

## Managing Notes

### Clear All Notes

**Warning:** This deletes everything permanently!

**Steps:**
1. Go to notes page
2. Click **"🗑️ Clear All Notes"**
3. Confirm in dialog
4. All notes deleted from localStorage

**Before clearing:**
- ✅ Export CSV as backup
- ✅ Ensure important candidates have been shared
- ✅ Team has reviewed notes if collaborating

### Individual Note Deletion

Currently not supported in UI. To delete individual notes:
1. Export JSON backup
2. Open browser DevTools (F12)
3. Go to Application → Local Storage
4. Find `candidate_matcher_notes`
5. Edit JSON manually
6. Save changes

(Advanced users only!)

---

## Best Practices

### When to Add Notes

**Always add notes when:**
- ✅ Candidate is a strong match (80%+)
- ✅ You have specific concerns
- ✅ Next steps are needed
- ✅ Salary/availability issues
- ✅ Referring to another role
- ✅ Scheduling interviews

**Optional for:**
- Low matches you're skipping
- Candidates clearly not qualified
- Duplicate applications

### What to Include

**Essential:**
- Key strengths relative to job
- Major gaps or concerns
- Availability timeline
- Salary expectation vs budget
- Recommended next step

**Helpful additions:**
- Culture fit assessment
- Communication style observed
- Technical depth in specific areas
- Comparison to other candidates
- Referral source quality

### Note Organization

**Structure suggestions:**

```
[Strengths]
- Skill A: Advanced level, 5 years
- Skill B: Working knowledge

[Concerns]
- Missing: Required skill C
- Salary: 15% above range

[Logistics]
- Availability: 2 weeks notice
- Location: Willing to relocate

[Recommendation]
→ Proceed to technical screen
→ Consider for senior role instead
→ Keep in pipeline for future opening
```

---

## Collaboration Workflows

### Sharing with Team

**Option 1: Export CSV**
1. Review candidates, add notes
2. Export CSV
3. Email to hiring manager/team
4. Discuss in meeting

**Option 2: Shared Screen**
1. Share GitHub Pages URL
2. Each team member reviews independently
3. Notes are per-person (not shared)
4. Consolidate manually

**Option 3: Assign Reviews**
1. Person A reviews candidates 1-20
2. Person B reviews candidates 21-40
3. Both export their notes
4. Merge CSVs in Excel

### Multi-Device Users

If you use multiple computers/browsers:

**Challenge:** Notes don't sync automatically

**Solutions:**
1. **Export/Import manually** (advanced)
   - Export JSON from device A
   - Manually merge with device B localStorage
   
2. **Designate primary device**
   - Always review on same computer
   - Use other devices for viewing only

3. **Use Google Sheets backend** (future feature)
   - Notes sync automatically
   - Requires setup

---

## Privacy & Security

### Data Privacy

- Notes **never leave your browser**
- Not sent to GitHub, servers, or third parties
- Only visible to you (on same browser)
- Deleted when localStorage cleared

### Security Considerations

**Safe for:**
- Internal candidate assessments
- Interview feedback
- Salary negotiation notes
- Team collaboration (with export)

**Not recommended for:**
- SSN or sensitive PII
- Background check results
- Compensation decisions (without export backup)
- Legal/compliance records (use official system)

### Compliance

For GDPR/CCPA compliance:
- Notes contain personal data (candidate info)
- Export and store securely if retaining
- Delete when no longer needed
- Don't include sensitive data types

---

## Troubleshooting

**Notes not saving:**
- Check browser allows localStorage
- Not in incognito/private mode
- Try different browser
- Clear cache and reload

**Notes disappeared:**
- Browser cache was cleared
- Different browser/device used
- Check localStorage quota exceeded
- Restore from CSV export if available

**Can't add more notes:**
- localStorage quota reached (~5-10MB)
- Export old notes and clear space
- Delete very old notes

**Want to sync across devices:**
- Currently not supported
- Export/import manually (advanced)
- Wait for Google Sheets integration (future)

---

## Advanced: Programmatic Access

### Read Notes via Console

```javascript
// Get all notes
const notes = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('candidate_matcher_notes'));

// Get notes for specific candidate
const candidateNotes = notes[123]; // replace with candidate_id

// Count total notes
const totalNotes = Object.values(notes).flat().length;
```

### Modify Notes via Console

```javascript
// Add a note programmatically
const notes = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('candidate_matcher_notes')) || {};

if (!notes[123]) notes[123] = [];

notes[123].push({
  note_text: 'Added via console',
  rating: 5,
  created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
  updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
});

localStorage.setItem('candidate_matcher_notes', JSON.stringify(notes));
```

### Backup Script

```javascript
// Download all notes as JSON file
const notes = localStorage.getItem('candidate_matcher_notes');
const blob = new Blob([notes], {type: 'application/json'});
const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
const a = document.createElement('a');
a.href = url;
a.download = `notes_backup_${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]}.json`;
a.click();
```

---

## Next Steps

- [User Guide](user-guide.qmd) - General usage
- [Matching Algorithm](matching-algorithm.qmd) - How scoring works
- [Data Export](data-export.qmd) - Weekly refresh process
